widgets_values:
do the template edits in the model loader group (save/delete) because it starts the workflow to create the file and stops it. you don't want to do this in the middle of the workflow ;) 

refresh the browser page to update the template list of all Smart Loaders to use the template somewhere else in the workflow.

if the image contains data like prompt it is transported within the txt_pos and txt_i2p slots. 

this allows to disable all nodes in the prompt area (using txt_pos) or use this prompt and advance it (txt_i2p). 

when the group image to prompt is enabled it overwrites txt_i2p!

the auto crop funktion works best on subjects that don't fill the whole frame in e.g. landscape format images, in cases like this use the manual crop funktion. set the crop/resize preview to "always pause" until you are happy with the crop. after that set the stop to no, cancel the current queue and do new queue.

the resize image v2 node is doin the final crop in case the image size don't fit the values you have setup in the project folder node.

JDCN AnyFileSelector has a "Change" Value, set it to increment to load every image in the folder e.g. with Auto Queue.

to use this group enable the load image group.

this group works without enabling one of the i2i options in the Initial Render group

Can be combined with Flux Depth Lora & PuLID Flux, works also in combination with a kontext checkpoint.

nodes.title: Detail Daemon: Settings
widgets_values:
### Fluxmania

| Kreamania fp8 |Detail Daemon Sampler | 
|-------------------|----|
| detail_amount | 0.25 |
| start         | 0.10 |
| end           | 0.80 |
| bias          | 0.50 |
| exponent      | 1,0  |
| start offset  | 0,00 |
| end offset    | 0,00 |
| fade          | 0 |
| Smooth          | True |
| CFG Scale Override  | 0 |


### CreArt

| 8Step HyperFlux |Detail Daemon Sampler | 
|-------------------|----|
| detail_amount | 0.51 |
| start         | 0.36 |
| end           | 0.80 |
| bias          | 0.15 |
| exponent      | 1,40 |
| start offset  | 0,07 |
| end offset    | -0,04 |
| fade          | 0 |
| Smooth          | True |
| CFG Scale Override  | 0 |


### My Standard

| Standard | Detail Daemon Sampler | 
|-------------------|----|
| detail_amount | 0.35 |
| start         | 0.20 |
| end           | 0.80  |
| bias          | 0.41 |
| exponent      | 0 |
| start offset  | 0 |
| end offset    | 0 |
| fade          | 0 |
| Smooth          | True |
| CFG Scale Override  | 1,0 |

nodes.title: Prompt (1 Prompt per Line)
widgets_values:
her mouth is open and reveals sharp fangs, remove the watermark
head_swap: start with Picture 1 as the base image, keeping its lighting, environment, and background. remove the head from Picture 1 completely and replace it with the head from Picture 2. ensure the head and body have correct anatomical proportions, and blend the skin tones, shadows, and lighting naturally so the final result appears as one coherent, realistic person.

the prompt node allows you to setup multible promtps and switch between them with the string from list node,
increase the index in string from list to select a different line.

important, just 1 prompt per line without line breaks.

for face/head swap use the lora:
https://civitai.com/models/2027766/bfs-best-face-swap-qwen-image-edit-2509, strength 1 works good.

standard qwen image edit returns better results, 4 step quality looks too bad imo.

nodes.title: 🐍 Prompt Preview
widgets_values: A cinematic film still of a woman lying on her stomach on a bed, with her back facing the viewer, showcasing her shiny, wet skin and long, flowing red hair. the focus is selectively placed on her backside, highlighting her curves and the texture of her skin, which is glistening with water droplets. she is wearing a gold choker and a gold bracelet, which adds a touch of elegance to her overall look. the woman appears to be in her mid-twenties, with a slim, toned body and a fair complexion. her long, wavy red hair cascades down her back, framing her face and accentuating her curves. the lighting is soft and warm, casting gentle shadows that highlight her smooth, glossy skin and create a sense of intimacy and sensuality. the background is blurred, allowing the focus to remain on the woman's body, making her the focal point of the image. the overall effect is one of sensuality and relaxation, with the woman lying down on a soft, white bedsheet, creating a peaceful and intimate atmosphere,dolly zoom, dramatic effect with moving camera and zoom, image in the style of Dave Seeley, dynamic and detailed fantasy illustration, chiaroscuro, strong contrasts between light and dark for dramatic effect, Anime, Japanese animation style with expressive characters and vibrant colors, Mystical Atmosphere, magical and otherworldly ambiance with ethereal elements, with shallow depth of field, vignette, highly detailed high budget production values, bokeh, cinemascope framing, moody and epic atmosphere, gorgeous film grain

nodes.title: Model links
widgets_values:
[Tutorial](https://docs.comfy.org/tutorials/image/qwen/qwen-image-edit) | [教程](https://docs.comfy.org/zh-CN/tutorials/image/qwen/qwen-image-edit)


## Model links

You can find all the models on [Comfy-Org/Qwen-Image_ComfyUI](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Qwen-Image_ComfyUI/tree/main) and  [Comfy-Org/Qwen-Image-Edit_ComfyUI](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Qwen-Image-Edit_ComfyUI) 

**Diffusion model**

- [qwen_image_edit_2509_fp8_e4m3fn.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Qwen-Image-Edit_ComfyUI/resolve/main/split_files/diffusion_models/qwen_image_edit_2509_fp8_e4m3fn.safetensors)

**LoRA**

- [Qwen-Image-Edit-2509-Lightning-4steps-V1.0-bf16.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/lightx2v/Qwen-Image-Lightning/resolve/main/Qwen-Image-Edit-2509/Qwen-Image-Edit-2509-Lightning-4steps-V1.0-bf16.safetensors)

**Text encoder**

- [qwen_2.5_vl_7b_fp8_scaled.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Qwen-Image_ComfyUI/resolve/main/split_files/text_encoders/qwen_2.5_vl_7b_fp8_scaled.safetensors)

**VAE**

- [qwen_image_vae.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Qwen-Image_ComfyUI/resolve/main/split_files/vae/qwen_image_vae.safetensors)

Model Storage Location

```
📂 ComfyUI/
├── 📂 models/
│   ├── 📂 diffusion_models/
│   │   └── qwen_image_edit_2509_fp8_e4m3fn.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 loras/
│   │   └── Qwen-Image-Lightning-4steps-V1.0.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 vae/
│   │   └── qwen_image_vae.safetensors
│   └── 📂 text_encoders/
│       └── qwen_2.5_vl_7b_fp8_scaled.safetensors
```

enable the Load Image group, load an image and use the preprocessor group.

when using Qwen Image Diffsynth: 
-> set everything in the Fast Muter to no and enable Qwen Image Diffsynth. 
Do not activate i2i (Qwen DiffSynth) in the initial render group! (This option is only used when the lora is loaded, e.g., in the smart loader, and not for this group.)

enable the Standard+Union ControlNet Loader and select the controlnet model that fits your checkpoint (SD15/SDXL/SD3/Flux)

for SDXL you can use the ControlNet++ Loader, select a Union ControlNet Model.
disable the Standard+Union Loader and enable the ControlNet++ Loader.

When Using a Flux Union Model, enable Flux Union Types (this enables the standard loader automaticly), select the Flux Union ControlNet Model.

for flux, i wouldn't recommend using controlnets. instead, i would use the loras, because they work much better and use less memory.
https://civitai.com/models/970050/flux-tools-depth-lora

leave the denoise in the initial render group at 1.0 to use the controlnet only.
when using the flux lora don't forget to enable the i2i (Flux Preproc) option in the initial render group.

When the Detailer Detection preview shows a detection for i.e. each eye but also a selection for both together, change the seed. this works most of the time. increment +1 because you don't want a selection of both. that means the nose and everything around the eyes will be detailed too wich looks wierd (depending on the model). both detections of each eye will be detailed combined to make sure that both eyes have the same color.

The Detailers have a Stop Options, if you notice a false detection e.g. when the complete image is surrounded with a red rectangle. use the stop button and change the seed until you have a correct detection of just the part the you want, sometimes it helps to switch the model, if you have tried different seeds and don't get the correct detection.
Sam2 is converting the box into a selection around the object using sam2 models. the stop also allows you to change the model or adjust the settings for sam2.

When you click on a subgraph group, you must switch to the “new menu” when using the legacy design. you can use the navigation in the top menu or press esc to return to the workflow (in the latest comfyui version you don't have to switch the mode, with esc you can return to the workflow in legacy mode too).

When using the stop function the workflow stops after the initial render. use a fixed seed before starting a new queue to keep the rendered image otherwise a new image is rendered if one of the seeds is set to random (image/prompt seed). this function allows you to setup the rest of the workflow for that image e.g. activating/bypassing detailers etc.

the checkoint loader in the detailers are optional, if bypassed the model loaded in the Model Loader group is used, adjust the sampler settings or enable allow_overwrite to use the values stored in the template.

Bypassing the Basic Guider will use the CFG Guider e.g. for distilled flux models or sdxl etc. models that requires a cfg value, enable the negative prompt in the prompt area if needed.

check if the noise injection works for the selected model, it takes a bit more time but most of the time it's worth it. you can change the values for Sigmas_Denoise and Noise_Strength to make it work ;)

i2i (Denoise): Image to Image 
-> use the load image group, change the denoise value in the sampler settings node to a lower value e.g. 0.50, disable noise injection

i2i (Flux Preproc): Image to Image with a preprocessor image as input (e.g. depth/canny) 
-> use the load image group, enable the preprocessor group, add the lora flux depth/canny with a value of e.g. 0.75, change the prepocessor model to either depth or canny/lineart

i2i (Flux Kontext): 
-> use a flux kontext checkpoint, use the model sampling flux patcher, CFG Zero Star (optional), the load image group, image to prompt for a description (optional).

i2i (Qwen DiffSynth): Image to Image with a preprocessor image as input (e.g. depth/canny)
-> use a qwen image checkpoint, the qwen image union diffsynth lora (model only, can be loaded in the smart loader), use the model sampling aura flow patcher, image to prompt for a description (optional), enable the preprocessor group and choose e.g. canny/depth. 
the implemented version works with 2 reference latents (pos/neg) and the lora instead of the controlnet node. the lora don't work with nunchaku models. i've also added the qwen image diffsynth in the controlnet group, don't use them together!

Enable only one of the i2i Options !!

In Smart Loader Plus, there is an option to save sampler values for the different models. The idea behind this is quite simple: I can't remember all the recommended settings for all the checkpoints I've downloaded, so I save them in the template. The node sampler settings in the Initial Render group allow you to overwrite the values set there with those stored in the template. To do this, turn on allow_overwrite.

if you run out of memory (OOM) at the detection / sam2 ultra nodes, hit queue/run again.

- to change the size of the logo change the value scale_to_length in the node: "scale logo". y=up/down, x=left/right

- to change the position change the x and/or y direction and the initial position in the node: "add logo to the image".

to use custom watermark, find a font that you like and copy it to the custom node folder:

ComfyUI\custom_nodes\comfyui_layerstyle\font

click refresh in the panel or restart comfyui

allura can be found here:
https://www.1001fonts.com/allura-font.html

in some cases you'll see some kind of border on the edges of the images from detailer: tiles, you can use the crop function to remove them before resizing the image. 10 pixel is enough. this issue seems to be gone with the use of the resize to round node in the upscale group but i leave it here anyways. it's also usable to crop out things.
her mouth is open and reveals sharp fangs, remove the watermark

nodes.title: Note: Installation
widgets_values:
👉🏻 if you don't have llama_cpp installed:

- python_embeded\python.exe -m pip install https://github.com/r-vage/ComfyUI_Eclipse/releases/download/wheels/llama_cpp_python-0.3.16-cp312-cp312-win_cu12.8_amd64.whl

👉🏻 models are downloaded automaticly on execution if not exist.
